title: Reprogramming White Fat Cells for Adipose Manipulation Transplantation Therapy

steps.title: To target promoters, use CRISPick [28]...
steps.instruction: To target promoters, use CRISPick [28] or other sgRNA design tool for CRISPRa, select your reference genome and type of Cas enzyme, enter the target gene name, and pick the top 5 sequences. For enhancers, you will need to upload the sequence you want to target.

steps.title: Order primers with flanking restriction enzyme...
steps.instruction: Order primers with flanking restriction enzyme sites and prepare 100 μM stock solutions using dH 2 O. a. sasgRNA_F-Inf- BstX I: gagaaCCAcctTGTTGG(your sgRNA-seq)gttAtagtactctgg b. sasgRNA_RevInf Xho I (universal): ggatccTAGTActcgagAAaaaaatctc

steps.title: PCR setup Step Temperature Time Initial...
steps.instruction: PCR setup Step Temperature Time Initial denaturation 98 °C 1 min Cycling 32 cycles 98 °C 10 s 60 °C 5 s 68 °C 2 s Final extension 68 °C 1 min (15–30 s per kb)

steps.title: Measure DNA concentration
steps.instruction: Measure DNA concentration.

steps.title: Prepare AAV backbone digestion mix (see...
steps.instruction: Prepare AAV backbone digestion mix (see Recipes) for 1–3 h of AAV backbone digestion using BstXI and XhoI.

steps.title: Run inserts and the digested vector...
steps.instruction: Run inserts and the digested vector on the 1% agarose gel. Visualize the gel and then excise the bands for the digested vector and sgRNA.

steps.title: Perform DNA extraction on the bands...
steps.instruction: Perform DNA extraction on the bands using the QIAquick Gel Extraction kit.

steps.title: On ice, set up NEBuilder ®...
steps.instruction: On ice, set up NEBuilder ® HiFi DNA assembly reaction (see Recipes).

steps.title: Incubate samples in a thermocycler at...
steps.instruction: Incubate samples in a thermocycler at 50 °C for 15 min, if 2 or 3 fragments are being assembled, or for 60 min, if 4–6 fragments are being assembled.

steps.title: Transform Stellar competent cells with 2...
steps.instruction: Transform Stellar competent cells with 2 μL of the chilled assembled product following Takara’s Stellar™ Competent Cells protocol PT5055-

steps.title: Pick colonies and inoculate overnight
steps.instruction: Pick colonies and inoculate overnight. a. Make LB medium containing ampicillin (1:

steps.title: and add 3 mL per 14 mL polyethylene round tube
steps.instruction: and add 3 mL per 14 mL polyethylene round tube. b. Pick one colony from the LB-ampicillin culture plate from your transformation step and add to each tube. c. Incubate in a bacterial shaking incubator at 37 °C with shaking at 250× g for 7–8 h. d. Continue to miniprep.

steps.title: Perform a plasmid miniprep with the QiaPrep Spin MiniPrep
steps.instruction: Perform a plasmid miniprep with the QiaPrep Spin MiniPrep.

steps.title: Send for sequencing to validate proper cloning of sgRNA
steps.instruction: Send for sequencing to validate proper cloning of sgRNA.

steps.title: Store your plasmids at -20 °C
steps.instruction: Store your plasmids at -20 °C. B. CRISPRa optimization via transfection in preadipocytes B

steps.title: Thaw cells and maintain preadipocytes below 80% confluency
steps.instruction: Thaw cells and maintain preadipocytes below 80% confluency. 0.4 mL, repeat the centrifugation.

steps.title: Discard the flowthrough and add 1...
steps.instruction: Discard the flowthrough and add 1 mL per initial 150 mm plate of suspension buffer in the Amicon Ultra column.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 2,000× g for 5 min at 15 °C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 2,000× g for 5 min at 15 °C. Repeat centrifugation if the volume is >0.4 mL after the spin.

steps.title: Repeat steps C3
steps.instruction: Repeat steps C3.15–16 four more times for a total of five washes.

steps.title: Discard the flowthrough
steps.instruction: Discard the flowthrough. Transfer the virus suspension in the column to a new 1.5 mL tube.

steps.title: Aliquot 2 μL for AAV titration
steps.instruction: Aliquot 2 μL for AAV titration. Store the virus at -80 °C.

steps.title: Use the AAVpro ® Titration kit (for real-time PCR) v
steps.instruction: Use the AAVpro ® Titration kit (for real-time PCR) v. 2 for AAV titration. D. Culturing preadipocytes for differentiation

steps.title: Thaw cells and maintain preadipocytes below 80% confluency
steps.instruction: Thaw cells and maintain preadipocytes below 80% confluency. Confluency higher than 85% can impact differentiation. Culture these cells with preadipocyte culturing medium.

steps.title: When ready to seed cells for differentiation
steps.instruction: When ready to seed cells for differentiation: a. Wash cells with DPBS. b. Use trypsin-EDTA and incubate in an incubator for 5 min to dissociate. c. Use preadipocyte culturing medium to neutralize trypsin. Collect cells in solution into a conical tube. d. Spin down cells in a centrifuge at 300× g for 5 min. e. Aspirate supernatant and resuspend the cell solution in 1 mL of preadipocyte culturing medium. f. Count cells.

steps.title: Check plated cells to make sure...
steps.instruction: Check plated cells to make sure they are at 100% confluency.

steps.title: Add adipocyte differentiation media
steps.instruction: Add adipocyte differentiation media.

steps.title: Place the plate back into the incubator
steps.instruction: Place the plate back into the incubator.

steps.title: Change media every other day for 14–21 days
steps.instruction: Change media every other day for 14–21 days. Change media using a pipette; do not use a vacuum aspirator, as it can disrupt adipocytes, which are more fragile due to the lipid droplets that will form. a. Adipocytes will start to form lipid droplets that look like clusters of small, empty/clear circular structures in the cell. E

steps.title: Twenty-four to forty-eight hours after seeding...
steps.instruction: Twenty-four to forty-eight hours after seeding human preadipocytes in a Nunclon Sphera plate, prepare adipocyte differentiation media (see Recipes) and warm to 37 °C.

steps.title: Observe that the cells have formed...
steps.instruction: Observe that the cells have formed spherical organoid structures at the bottom of the wells. Using a pipette, carefully remove the preadipocyte growth media. Work with caution so that you do not suck up the organoids.

steps.title: Add 100 µL of adipocyte differentiation media to each well
steps.instruction: Add 100 µL of adipocyte differentiation media to each well.

steps.title: Change media every day for 21–28 days by pipetting
steps.instruction: Change media every day for 21–28 days by pipetting. Pipette out half of the media and add 50 μL of new differentiation media. a. Be mindful that evaporation can occur, so removing half of the media can be less than 50 μL. b. Be cautious of sucking up adipose organoids, which are small and in suspension. F. CRISPRa browning of human adipocytes or adipose organoids F

steps.title: Transfecting adipocytes
steps.instruction: Transfecting adipocytes

steps.title: On day 4 of adipocyte differentiation,...
steps.instruction: On day 4 of adipocyte differentiation, change the cell media (adipocyte differentiation media).

steps.title: Prepare an adequate amount of CRISPRa...
steps.instruction: Prepare an adequate amount of CRISPRa transfection mix for the number of wells you are working with (see Recipes).

steps.title: Incubate transfection mix for 15 min at room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate transfection mix for 15 min at room temperature.

steps.title: Add the appropriate amount of the...
steps.instruction: Add the appropriate amount of the mixture dropwise into each well.

steps.title: Place the culture plate on the...
steps.instruction: Place the culture plate on the magnetofection magnetic plate for 30 min.

steps.title: Change media 6 h after transfection or the next day
steps.instruction: Change media 6 h after transfection or the next day. F

steps.title: For adipocytes, after day 2 of...
steps.instruction: For adipocytes, after day 2 of adipocyte differentiation, add AAVs with an MOI of 1 × 10 6 .

steps.title: For adipose organoids, if grafting for cancer therapy
steps.instruction: For adipose organoids, if grafting for cancer therapy: One week before grafting adipose organoids, treat organoids with AAVs with an MOI of 1 × 10 6 .

description: Validation of protocol All validation data and information about the analysis are included in the original research article: Nguyen et al. [27] Implantation of engineered adipocytes suppresses tumor progression in cancer models. Nature Biotechnology . The original research article confirms browning activation in white adipocytes after CRISPR activation (Extended Data Figure 1, Nguyen et al. [27]). It also shows that oxygen consumption rates, glucose uptake, and fatty acid oxidation are increased in these CRISPRa-modulated adipocytes (Extended Data Figure 1, Nguyen et al. [27]). Additionally, the protocol to reprogram adipocytes is shown in [27] to be an effective method of metabolic therapy to target cancer. Cancer cell growth is shown to be suppressed when co-cultured with the CRISPRa reprogrammed human adipocytes (Figure 1, Nguyen et al. [27]). Glucose uptake of cancer cells is also reduced when these cells are co-cultured with CRISPRa-modulated human adipocytes (Figure 1, Nguyen et al. [27]). Cancer cells also exhibit decreased glucose utilization, as shown by measurements of the extracellular acidification rate (ECAR) and metabolic gene expression (Figure 1, Nguyen et al. [27]). Using this protocol, UCP1 -CRISPRa-engineered adipose organoids were generated and co-transplanted in mice to suppress the growth of xenografted tumors (Figure 2, Nguyen et al. [27]).
